version_date: Gazetted 11 May 2011 (Government Gazette No. 77); revokes SPP 3.2 Planning for Aboriginal Communities gazetted 11 August 2000
scope_summary: State Planning Policy made under Part 3 of the Planning and Development Act 2005 providing for the recognition and orderly, coordinated development of Aboriginal settlements throughout Western Australia, primarily in remote regions. Its core mechanism is the WAPC-endorsed Layout Plan (map-set, background report and provisions) prepared in consultation with resident communities, traditional owners and local government, with local planning schemes to classify endorsed settlement areas as a 'settlement' zone. It does not apply to mining camps, tourism sites or working pastoral settlements.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: place qualifying as an Aboriginal settlement under the policy

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 3 Urban Growth and Settlement
State Planning Policy 1 State Planning Framework Policy
State Planning Policy No. 2.7 Public Drinking Water Source Policy
State Planning Policy 3.2 Planning for Aboriginal Communities (2000, revoked)
Corporations (Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander) Act 2006
